title: DAG build reports status=succeeded despite systemic provider outage dropping high-severity behaviors (false green)
description: When the coding loop cannot complete an issue and the recovery Issue Advisor also fails, the DAG records an 'accept_with_debt' adaptation and continues; the overall build then returns status=succeeded with completed_issues counted and failed_issues=0, even when the failures are a systemic provider/auth outage rather than genuine per-issue difficulty. In build exec_20260616_151835, a mid-run Max-plan OAuth expiry caused ~347 provider exit-1s; 6 high-severity behaviors (meetings-routes-api/B4, transcript-guard/B5, meeting-kind-sessions/B6, recall-bot-route/B7, webhook-meeting-provision/B10, backend-integration) were silently dropped as debt, no PR was opened (pr_url=''), yet the build reported 'succeeded'. accept_with_debt also masks the distinction between 'this issue is hard' and 'every provider call is failing'. Fix: detect systemic failure (e.g. provider error rate over a threshold, or repeated identical AuthError) and abort the build with status=failed; and/or treat N accumulated high-severity 'missing_functionality' debts (or zero PR) as a non-success terminal status.
design: DAG executor accept_with_debt path (swe_af/execution/dag_executor.py) + build() terminal status in swe_af/app.py. Add: (1) circuit-breaker on consecutive/identical provider errors (esp. AuthError from SWE-AF-vvk) -> abort; (2) severity-aware terminal status: high-sev debt or empty PR => completed_with_debt/failed, not succeeded.
acceptance_criteria: A build that drops >=1 high-severity behavior as debt AND/OR opens no PR does NOT return status=succeeded; a run where provider auth fails repeatedly aborts early with status=failed and a clear cause; build result distinguishes 'completed', 'completed_with_debt', and 'failed'.
notes: Depends on SWE-AF-vvk for clean AuthError classification. Evidence: exec_20260616_151835 result.dag_state: completed_issues=20, failed_issues=0, accumulated_debt=6 (all high), pr_url='' yet status=succeeded.
close_reason: Fixed: fatal provider/auth failures abort the DAG, BuildResult now reports completed/completed_with_debt/failed, and high-severity debt or missing expected PR is non-success.

title: ClaudeCodeProvider swallows subprocess stdout/stderr on exit-1 (401 reported as '(no stderr captured)')
description: The claude_code runtime drives the bundled claude binary (claude_agent_sdk/_bundled/claude). When that subprocess exits non-zero, the harness logs only 'ClaudeCodeProvider error: Command failed with exit code 1', 'Fatal error in message reader: Command failed with exit code 1', and 'Error output: Check stderr output for details / Stderr output: (no stderr captured)'. The actual cause is emitted by the binary on STDOUT (e.g. 'API Error: 401 {"type":"authentication_error","message":"Invalid authentication credentials"} · Please run /login'), which the harness discards. In build exec_20260616_151835 a Max-plan OAuth token expired mid-run; this surfaced as ~347 opaque exit-1 errors with no diagnosable reason, costing a full diagnosis pass. Fix: capture and surface the subprocess STDOUT (and stderr) on non-zero exit; detect auth errors (401/'Please run /login') and fail fast with a clear AuthError instead of generic retries.
design: Provider/transport in claude_agent_sdk message reader + swe_af/runtime/codex_harness_patch.py wrapping. Tee child stdout; on rc!=0 attach last output to the raised error. Add a regex classifier for 'API Error: 401' / 'Please run /login'.
acceptance_criteria: On provider subprocess exit!=0, the captured stdout+stderr (or first N KB) is logged at error level and included in the reasoner failure; a 401/authentication_error is classified distinctly (e.g. AuthExpiredError) and short-circuits retries with an actionable message.
notes: Evidence: build exec_20260616_151835 (node swe-baml-meetings, image swe-af-baml:smoke). Repro: run _bundled/claude -p 'Reply OK' --model haiku with an expired CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN -> prints 401 on stdout, exit 1.
close_reason: Fixed: Claude Code subprocess stdout is retained on non-zero exit, auth banners are classified as AuthError, and fatal auth results avoid schema retries.

title: Implement BAML-typed deterministic backpressure rung (B0–B7)
description: Execute approved TDD plan thoughts/searchable/shared/plans/2026-06-11-07-43-tdd-baml-typed-deterministic-backpressure.md on feat/baml-structured-output. Typed AcceptanceCheck + Pydantic validator + manifest detector + run_local_check + resolution + gated coding-loop rung; B0a bridge dict/Any hardening; B6b output_format render; B7 planner emits field. RGR per behavior; commit per vertical slice.
close_reason: All 10 behaviors (B0–B7, B6b) implemented TDD with full suite green (1109 passed). 3 deferred-verify items (live-LLM/real-build) tracked in follow-up.

title: Plan Review: BAML-Typed Deterministic Backpressure — 3 critical fixes before impl
description:
Pre-implementation review of thoughts/searchable/shared/plans/2026-06-11-07-43-tdd-baml-typed-deterministic-backpressure.md. Verified all file:line citations against /home/maceo/Dev/SWE-AF-baml @ 6517b17. Verdict: Needs Minor-to-Moderate Revision. Full report: ...-REVIEW.md.

CRITICAL (must fix before impl):
1) Behavior 4 runner contract mismatch: ci_gate.CommandRunner is argv-shaped (Sequence[str]), cannot run shell pipelines. Wrap as ['bash','-lc',cmd] (reuses argv type) or define distinct ShellRunner.
2) Behavior 4 timeout: ci_gate has NO per-subprocess timeout/-1 sentinel to 'mirror'. Must add subprocess.run(timeout=...) + TimeoutExpired->passed=False, else a hung command blocks the build forever (breaks the 'bounded' promise).
3) Behavior 6 control flow: run_coding_loop has no continue/break; a raw 'continue' bypasses checkpoint(730)+memory(737-745)+history. Gate the if-needs_deeper_qa branch and fall through; never continue.

WARNINGS: checkpoint persists summary not feedback (fold tail into summary); det_check_attempts must be added to checkpoint+resume loader; BAML @check has no fluent API (B0 spike covers it); PlannedIssue insertion anchors drift (use named anchors); config triplication propagation untested (add BuildConfig->ExecutionConfig flag test); manifest-fallback whole-suite pytest false-reds (exit 5 = no tests collected).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-11: all 3 critical + 6 warnings folded into the plan in place (plan grew 594->687 lines). B4: argv CommandRunner reused via [bash,-c,cmd] + own deterministic_check_timeout_seconds=600 + TimeoutExpired->exit_code=-1. B6: rung gates reviewer branch and falls through (no continue), folds tail into persisted summary, persists det_check_attempts across resume, +propagation test, extra=forbid semantics. Warnings: _tail re-export, BAML @check->tb.add_baml raw, named anchors for PlannedIssue/verification, pytest exit-5 false-red documented in B5. REVIEW status flipped to Ready for Implementation.
close_reason: Findings applied to plan; pre-impl review complete

title: Follow-up: live-LLM verification of backpressure rung + output_format rollout
description: Deferred-verify items from the BAML deterministic backpressure plan (epic SWE-AF-x2g), each needing a live LLM key/build: (1) B6 manual — observe a real coder iteration re-iterate on a real test failure; (2) B7 key-gated integration — a real planner run emits a populated verification[]; (3) B6b measured rollout — A/B output_format emission compliance, then wire config.enable_baml_output_format -> codex_harness_patch.baml_output_format_enabled contextvar and consider defaulting on.
